structuredAttrs.buildCommand:
if [ ! -x "/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox" ]
then
    echo "cannot find executable file \`/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox'"
    exit 1
fi

#########################
#                       #
#   EXTRA PREF CHANGES  #
#                       #
#########################
# Link the runtime. The executable itself has to be copied,
# because it will resolve paths relative to its true location.
# Any symbolic links have to be replicated as well.
cd "/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1"
find . -type d -exec mkdir -p "$out"/{} \;

find . -type f \( -not -name "firefox" \) -exec ln -sT "/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1"/{} "$out"/{} \;

find . -type f \( -name "firefox" -o -name "firefox-bin" \) -print0 | while read -d $'\0' f; do
  cp -P --no-preserve=mode,ownership --remove-destination "/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1/$f" "$out/$f"
  chmod a+rwx "$out/$f"
done

# fix links and absolute references

find . -type l -print0 | while read -d $'\0' l; do
  target="$(readlink "$l")"
  target=${target/#"/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1"/"$out"}
  ln -sfT "$target" "$out/$l"
done

cd "$out"

cd "Applications/Firefox.app"

# The omni.ja files have to be copied and not symlinked, otherwise tabs crash.
# Maybe related to how omni.ja file is mmapped into memory. See:
# https://github.com/mozilla/gecko-dev/blob/b1662b447f306e6554647914090d4b73ac8e1664/modules/libjar/nsZipArchive.cpp#L204
#
# Mach-O shared libraries must be copied, not symlinked, otherwise some
# functionality like the Crypto API and audio decoding is broken.
find . -type l -print0 |
while IFS= read -r -d "" file; do
  case "$(basename "$file")" in
    omni.ja)
      ;;
    *)
      # Copy if the symlink resolves to a Mach-O dylib
      otool -l "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'LC_ID_DYLIB' || continue
      ;;
  esac

  rm "$file"
  cp "/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1/Applications/Firefox.app/${file#./}" "$file"
done

# Copy any embedded .app directories; plugin-container fails to start otherwise.
for dir in $(find . -type d -name '*.app'); do
  rm -r "$dir"
  cp -r "/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1/Applications/Firefox.app/$dir" "$dir"
done

cd ..


# create the wrapper

executablePrefix="$out/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS"
executablePath="$out/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox"
oldWrapperArgs=()

if [[ -L $executablePath ]]; then
  # Symbolic link: wrap the link's target.
  oldExe="$(readlink -v --canonicalize-existing "$executablePath")"
  rm "$executablePath"
elif wrapperCmd=$(/nix/store/wgzxpvflx8nqj2vrlrc4z5v6wp9ckvpi-extract-binary-wrapper-cmd "$executablePath"); [[ $wrapperCmd ]]; then
  # If the executable is a binary wrapper, we need to update its target to
  # point to $out, but we can't just edit the binary in-place because of length
  # issues. So we extract the command used to create the wrapper and add the
  # arguments to our wrapper.
  parseMakeCWrapperCall() {
    shift # makeCWrapper
    oldExe=$1; shift
    oldWrapperArgs=("$@")
  }
  eval "parseMakeCWrapperCall ${wrapperCmd//"/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1"/"$out"}"
  rm "$executablePath"
else
  if read -rn2 shebang < "$executablePath" && [[ $shebang == '#!' ]]; then
    # Shell wrapper: patch in place to point to $out.
    sed -i "s@/nix/store/fzjwwvfr2ihcljcgb9dyrdw6wx4j59kq-firefox-unwrapped-150.0.1@$out@g" "$executablePath"
  fi
  # Suffix the executable with -old, because -wrapped might already be used by the old wrapper.
  oldExe="$executablePrefix/.firefox"-old
  mv "$executablePath" "$oldExe"
fi
concatTo makeWrapperArgs oldWrapperArgs

makeWrapper "$oldExe" "$out/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/MacOS/firefox" "${makeWrapperArgs[@]}"

#############################
#                           #
#   END EXTRA PREF CHANGES  #
#                           #
#############################
mkdir -p $out/lib/mozilla/native-messaging-hosts
for ext in ; do
    ln -sLt $out/lib/mozilla/native-messaging-hosts $ext/lib/mozilla/native-messaging-hosts/*
done

mkdir -p $out/lib/mozilla/pkcs11-modules
for ext in ; do
    ln -sLt $out/lib/mozilla/pkcs11-modules $ext/lib/mozilla/pkcs11-modules/*
done


#########################
#                       #
#   EXTRA PREF CHANGES  #
#                       #
#########################
# user customization
libDir="$out/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/Resources"

# creating policies.json
mkdir -p "$libDir/distribution"

POL_PATH="$libDir/distribution/policies.json"
rm -f "$POL_PATH"
cat /nix/store/dfv8xhy7w0mhgz6myy5mm1fg6msw39s0-policies.json >> "$POL_PATH"

extraPoliciesFiles=()
for extraPoliciesFile in "${extraPoliciesFiles[@]}"; do
  jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' $extraPoliciesFile "$POL_PATH" > .tmp.json
  mv .tmp.json "$POL_PATH"
done

# preparing for autoconfig
prefsDir="$out/Applications/Firefox.app/Contents/Resources/browser/defaults/preferences"
mkdir -p "$prefsDir"

echo 'pref("general.config.filename", "mozilla.cfg");' > "$prefsDir/autoconfig.js"
echo 'pref("general.config.obscure_value", 0);' >> "$prefsDir/autoconfig.js"

cat > "$libDir/mozilla.cfg" << EOF
// First line must be a comment

// Disables addon signature checking
// to be able to install addons that do not have an extid
// Security is maintained because only user whitelisted addons
// with a checksum can be installed


EOF

extraPrefsFiles=()
for extraPrefsFile in "${extraPrefsFiles[@]}"; do
  cat "$extraPrefsFile" >> "$libDir/mozilla.cfg"
done

cat >> "$libDir/mozilla.cfg" << EOF

EOF

mkdir -p "$libDir/distribution/extensions"

#############################
#                           #
#   END EXTRA PREF CHANGES  #
#                           #
#############################
structuredAttrs.name: firefox-150.0.1
